settingsHub.title: Settings Hub
settingsHub.customModelDesc: Manage OpenAI-compatible endpoints, private gateways, and locally deployed models. The model list refreshes automatically after saving.
settingsHub.proxyDesc: Configure HTTP, HTTPS, or SOCKS proxies. Saved settings take effect immediately for main process network requests—no need to manually edit .env.
settingsHub.skillsDesc: Maintain the global skills directory, handling imports, creation, and editing. The fixed directory is ~/.open-jarvis/skills.
settingsHub.mcpDesc: Manage the MCP Server list and configure default tool capabilities for all sessions.

general.generalSettingsDesc: Notifications, theme, language, and other basic configurations
general.themeDesc: Switch between light and dark appearance
general.languageDesc: Change the display language of the interface
general.desktopNotificationDesc: Show system notification center alerts for task completion and approval requests
general.notificationSoundDesc: Play task completion and approval sounds independently from desktop notifications
general.taskCompleteSoundDesc: Sound played when an agent task finishes
general.permissionRequestSoundDesc: Sound played when human approval is needed

exportDialog.title: Export Global Configuration
exportDialog.description: Export all configurations (models, proxy, MCP, skills) as a JSON file for backup or migration to other devices.
exportDialog.includeApiKeysDesc: The exported file will include all saved keys
exportDialog.noApiKeysWarning: API keys will not be included in the export file; you will need to reconfigure them after importing.

importDialog.title: Import Global Configuration
importDialog.description: Import configuration from a JSON file. Choose merge mode to keep existing settings, or replace mode to overwrite everything.
importDialog.mergeDesc: Keep existing configuration, only add or update imported items
importDialog.replaceDesc: Clear all existing configuration and completely overwrite with imported data
importDialog.replaceWarning: Replace mode will delete all existing configuration. This action cannot be undone.

importResult.title: Import Complete
importResult.description: Configuration imported successfully. The model list has been refreshed.

openAICompatible.title: Custom Model Configuration
openAICompatible.existingConfigsDesc: View, edit, and delete currently connected custom model configurations. Supports both OpenAI and Anthropic format endpoints.
openAICompatible.editorDesc: Fill in the request format, Base URL, API key, model ID, thinking parameters, and context window. After saving, it will immediately appear in the model list and be used for context window display and compression threshold calculations.
openAICompatible.fillBaseUrlAndModel: Please fill in the Base URL and Model ID
openAICompatible.duplicatedDraft: Copied as a new draft configuration; it takes effect after saving
openAICompatible.deleteModelWarning: This will delete the model configuration "{{name}}". This action cannot be undone.
openAICompatible.clickToEditOrAdd: Click a configuration on the left to edit, or click the add configuration button
openAICompatible.requestFormatDesc: Select the request body format compatible with your gateway. OpenAI format sends `thinking` and `reasoning_effort`; Anthropic format sends `thinking` and `output_config.effort`, with historical thinking/signature automatically preserved as content blocks by the official SDK.
openAICompatible.apiKeyPlaceholder: Can use a placeholder if the gateway does not require a key
openAICompatible.apiKeyToggleDesc: Click the button on the right to toggle between plaintext and masked key display.
openAICompatible.thinkingEffortDesc: Preserves the low, medium, high, xhigh/max editing levels. When sent to a compatible gateway, low/medium/high are normalized to high, and xhigh/max are normalized to max.
openAICompatible.reasoningAutoDesc: In auto mode, assistant.reasoning_content is passed back when historical tool calls are detected and thinking mode is enabled. Suitable for OpenAI-compatible models like MiMo that require multi-turn reasoning content replenishment.
openAICompatible.reasoningAnthropicDesc: In Anthropic mode, the official SDK passes back historical thinking/signature content blocks as-is; this switch does not intervene additionally.
openAICompatible.contextWindowDesc: It is recommended to fill in the model's actual maximum input tokens. Custom models like DeepSeek, Qwen, GLM, and MiniMax will prioritize this value; if left blank, it falls back to auto-inference.

mcpConfig.title: MCP Configuration
mcpConfig.defaultEnabledDesc: When checked, subsequent sessions will default to loading the corresponding MCP tools when calling the agent.
mcpConfig.noServers: No MCP servers available to enable.
mcpConfig.importExportDesc: Supports pasting Claude Desktop-style mcpServers JSON.
mcpConfig.deleteMcpWarning: This will delete MCP server "{{name}}". This action cannot be undone.

proxyConfig.title: Proxy Configuration
proxyConfig.description: After saving, the configuration will be written to the app environment and immediately update the global network proxy in the main process. Usually only the HTTPS proxy is needed.
proxyConfig.systemDesc: Use the system's default proxy settings. No manual configuration required.
proxyConfig.autoNodeProxy: The current implementation automatically writes NODE_USE_ENV_PROXY=1 when any proxy address is set, keeping subprocesses and some runtimes consistent.
proxyConfig.savedAndActive: Proxy configuration saved and active immediately

apiKey.updateDesc: Enter a new key to replace the existing one, or use the button below to remove it.
apiKey.addDesc: Enter the API key for {{name}} to use its models.
apiKey.providerDesc: Once a platform key is set, that provider's models can be called directly within this app.
apiKey.emptyNoOverwrite: Leaving blank will not overwrite the existing key.
apiKey.localOnly: The key is stored locally only and will not appear in session messages.

skills.title: Skills Configuration
skills.globalSourcesDesc: Skill sources are fixed to the global directory, using only ~/.open-jarvis/skills.
skills.defaultSourceOnly: Currently only using the default global skill source.
skills.skillFoldersDesc: Click a card to edit directly. Supports multi-select deletion and paginated browsing. New skills are created in a separate sub-dialog.
skills.selectWorkspaceFirst: Please select a global workspace in settings first, then you can import, edit, and create skills.
skills.noSkillFolders: The current global directory has no skill folders yet.
skills.noMatchingSkills: No matching skill folders. Try a different keyword.
skills.cardHint: Click a card to open the edit dialog and modify the folder name and SKILL.md content.
skills.editorDesc: Modify the skill folder name and SKILL.md content. Saving will create or rename the directory and write the document.
skills.skillFolderNamePlaceholder: Skill folder name (will be normalized to lowercase and hyphens)
skills.deleteSkillsWarning: This will permanently delete the selected {{count}} skill folders and their files. This action cannot be undone.
skills.needCustomModel: Please add at least one custom model configuration (Base URL and Model ID)
skills.confirmDeleteDesc: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
skills.deleteFolderWarning: This will remove the skill folder "{{name}}". This action cannot be undone.

about.title: About Open-Jarvis
about.description: Desktop intelligent agent interface based on deepagents
about.licenseDesc: This project is released under the Apache 2.0 license
about.toolingDesc: Embedded runtime versions bundled with the app

usage.title: Token Usage
usage.description: LLM token usage statistics across models and sessions
usage.chartDescription: Visualize token usage by model and recent activity
usage.noLogs: No token usage data recorded yet
